Package org.eventb.pptrans.tests

Class ReorganisationTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.pptrans.tests.AbstractTranslationTests
org.eventb.pptrans.tests.ReorganisationTests
public class ReorganisationTests extends AbstractTranslationTests
Unit testing of class org.eventb.internal.pp.translator.Reorganizer.

    Method
    Description
    void
    testCardExtractArithmeticExpression()
    Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.
    void
    testCardExtractBound()
    Ensures that extraction of a cardinal doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.
    void
    testCardExtractEqualExpr()
    Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.
    void

    Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.
    void

    Ensures that cardinals are extracted recursively.
    void

    Ensures that a cardinal is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.

    Ensures that a function application is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.

    Ensures that extraction of a function application doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.
    void

    Ensures that a function application is extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.
    void
    testFuncExtractRecursive()
    Ensures that function applications are extracted recursively.
    void

    Ensures that a function application is not extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.
    void
    testFuncNoExtractEqualIdent()
    Ensures that a function application is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.
    void
    testMinMaxExtractArithmeticExpression()
    Ensures that a x=min(A)imum or maximum is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.
    void
    testMinMaxExtractBound()
    Ensures that extraction of a minimum or maximum doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.
    void
    testMinMaxExtractEqualExpr()
    Ensures that a minimum or maximum is extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.
    void
    testMinMaxExtractRecursive()
    Ensures that minimums and maximums are extracted recursively.
    void
    testMinMaxNoExtractEqualIdent()
    Ensures that a minimum or maximum is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.
    void
    testMinMaxNoExtractInequality()
    Ensures that a minimum or maximum is not extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.

  • Method Details

    • testFuncNoExtractEqualIdent

      public void testFuncNoExtractEqualIdent()
      Ensures that a function application is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.

    • testFuncNoExtractEqualExpr

      public void testFuncNoExtractEqualExpr()
      Ensures that a function application is not extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.

    • testFuncExtractInequality

      public void testFuncExtractInequality()
      Ensures that a function application is extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.

    • testFuncExtractArithmeticExpression

      public void testFuncExtractArithmeticExpression()
      Ensures that a function application is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.

    • testFuncExtractRecursive

      public void testFuncExtractRecursive()
      Ensures that function applications are extracted recursively. We do it in only one context (from the right-hand side of an inequality), as other context should behave the same (this is checked with the simple tests).

    • testFuncExtractBound

      public void testFuncExtractBound()
      Ensures that extraction of a function application doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.

    • testCardNoExtractEqualIdent

      public void testCardNoExtractEqualIdent()
      Ensures that a cardinal is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.

    • testCardExtractEqualExpr

      public void testCardExtractEqualExpr()
      Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.

    • testCardExtractInequality

      public void testCardExtractInequality()
      Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.

    • testCardExtractArithmeticExpression

      public void testCardExtractArithmeticExpression()
      Ensures that a cardinal is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.

    • testCardExtractRecursive

      public void testCardExtractRecursive()
      Ensures that cardinals are extracted recursively. We do it in only one context (from the right-hand side of an inequality), as other context should behave the same (this is checked with the simple tests). Note that we do not give the full expansion, as it gets very complex. The fact that all cardinals have been translated is verified by checking that the result is in the goal.

    • testCardExtractBound

      public void testCardExtractBound()
      Ensures that extraction of a cardinal doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.

    • testMinMaxNoExtractEqualIdent

      public void testMinMaxNoExtractEqualIdent()
      Ensures that a minimum or maximum is not extracted when equalled to an identifier.

    • testMinMaxExtractEqualExpr

      public void testMinMaxExtractEqualExpr()
      Ensures that a minimum or maximum is extracted when equalled to an arithmetic expression which is not an identifier.

    • testMinMaxNoExtractInequality

      public void testMinMaxNoExtractInequality()
      Ensures that a minimum or maximum is not extracted when occurring on the side of an inequality.

    • testMinMaxExtractArithmeticExpression

      public void testMinMaxExtractArithmeticExpression()
      Ensures that a x=min(A)imum or maximum is extracted when occurring inside an arithmetic expression.

    • testMinMaxExtractRecursive

      public void testMinMaxExtractRecursive()
      Ensures that minimums and maximums are extracted recursively. We do it in only one context (from the right-hand side of an inequality), as other context should behave the same (this is checked with the simple tests). Note that we do not give the full expansion, as it gets very complex. The fact that all minimums and maximums have been translated is verified by checking that the result is in the goal.

    • testMinMaxExtractBound

      public void testMinMaxExtractBound()
      Ensures that extraction of a minimum or maximum doesn't break the de Bruijn coding of bound identifiers.

    • testComplex

      public void testComplex()

    • testComplex2

      public void testComplex2()

    • testComplex3

      public void testComplex3()
